public_reputation: Known around Shirshal through day-to-day work as porter.
appearance: Luneth Coilspire carries the marks of porter in ink, rain, and polished cloth: practical wear, maintained details, and a look that suggests they expect to be read before they are trusted.
manner: Judges the world by weight, delay, and whether moving one thing will jam five others behind it. In Shirshal, that habit reads through the public pressures of the place rather than decorative flourish.
daily_function: Performs the ongoing duties of porter in and around Shirshal, where the role has practical consequences even when nothing dramatic is happening.
current_agenda: Protect their footing in Shirshal while steering current strain away from a public rupture they may not survive politically or materially.
hidden_pressure: A private obligation, debt, promise, or fear tied to reputational exposure is narrowing their options faster than they admit.

adventure_hooks.summary: Luneth Coilspire can draw the party into a locality problem centered on porter duties in Shirshal.
Luneth Coilspire becomes useful when the party needs grounded help with magical law violations around Shirshal.

support_scope_notes: Created in V33_1 to ensure every locality-facing NPC access role resolves to a proper named person while remaining sparse and canon-safe.
public_signal: In public, Luneth Coilspire is immediately readable as a porter tied to Shirshal.

encounter_context.usual_environment: Shirshal: ordinary workspaces, public counters, roads, docks, shrines, workshops, or other role-relevant spaces
who_seeks_them_out: people who need access or clarity

first_impression: Luneth Coilspire presents as someone shaped by the duties of a porter and the daily pressures of Shirshal.
what_they_want_from_strangers: Useful clarity, respect for the work of a porter, and proof that strangers will not make Shirshal harder to manage.
what_they_refuse: Luneth Coilspire refuses careless disrespect, vague promises, and demands that threaten standing or workload.
how_they_create_problems: By enforcing the expectations of a porter too rigidly, protecting local reputation, or turning a small request into a larger obligation.
how_they_can_help: Can move people or goods quickly and knows which cargo draws the wrong attention.
escalation_if_crossed: If crossed, Luneth Coilspire adds scrutiny, delay, and locality-specific friction before direct punishment.
rumor_about_them: People say Luneth Coilspire always knows more about Shirshal than is immediately visible.
best_first_scene: A first encounter works best when the party needs guidance, access, or relief tied to a porter in Shirshal.
social_texture: Luneth Coilspire speaks like someone who has spent too long translating private stakes into acceptable public language. Around Shirshal, people read them as socially alert, procedural, and sensitive to status.
money_pressure: Their material pressure is tied to favors and tariffs; even when wealthy, they think in terms of loss, leverage, and what one bad week would expose.
status_anxiety: What they fear most is not simple failure but reputational exposure becoming socially visible before they can contain it.
long_memory: They remember who made their work harder, who preserved dignity under strain, and who tried to force speed without bearing consequence.
obvious_tell: When pressure rises, Luneth touches clothing, tools, or insignia before answering, as if checking that their role is still intact.
best_use_case: Use Luneth Coilspire when the party needs a socially grounded face for Shirshal: access, warning, delay, price, or reluctant help.
private_contradiction: They want to appear consistent, but their real choices are split between duty, self-protection, and preserving one relationship they cannot afford to lose.
